findings.title: Firestarter Backdoor — Cisco ASA/FTD Firmware-Level Persistence Survives Patches (CISA+NCSC Joint Alert)
findings.affected_technology: Cisco Adaptive Security Appliance (ASA), Cisco Firepower Threat Defense (FTD)
findings.summary: CISA and NCSC issued a joint alert after discovering that threat actor UAT-4356 deployed firmware-level backdoor malware on Cisco ASA/FTD firewalls that survives security patches, requiring full device reimaging for remediation.

findings.title: TeamPCP Supply Chain Expands — Bitwarden CLI npm Package Compromised via Checkmarx GitHub Action
findings.summary: TeamPCP leveraged a compromised Checkmarx GitHub Action to inject credential-stealing malware with self-propagation capabilities into the Bitwarden CLI npm package for 90 minutes on April 22.

findings.title: CVE-2026-33626 — LMDeploy Vision-Language Module SSRF Exploited Within 13 Hours of Disclosure
findings.summary: Server-side request forgery in LMDeploy's vision-language module enables unauthenticated access to cloud metadata services and internal networks, with exploitation observed within 13 hours of disclosure.

findings.title: CVE-2026-25895 — FUXA SCADA HMI Unauthenticated Path Traversal to RCE (PoC Public)
findings.summary: Public exploit chains unauthenticated path traversal with arbitrary file write to achieve remote code execution on internet-facing FUXA SCADA HMI servers.

noted.summary: Critical flaw (CVSS 9.9) in open-source no-code database application builder; details from dbugs.ptsecurity.com only, awaiting vendor advisory.
Critical vulnerability (CVSS 9.9) reported by PT Security; no vendor advisory or technical details published yet.
Web hosting control panel vulnerability (CVSS 8.8); internet-facing by design, limited details available.
Authentication bypass via patient social-login REST endpoint in versions up to 4.1.2; PoC on GitHub.
Path traversal via symlink extraction enabling arbitrary file overwrite; supply chain implications for pipelines using npm tar.
Critical vulnerability patched with no public technical details; monitor for advisory disclosure.
High-severity flaw patched with no public technical details; monitor for advisory disclosure.
VU#748485: Unauthenticated configuration modification vulnerability published by CERT/CC on April 23.
